root_cause: The `web` service was deployed with RELEASE=v2, which introduced a regression that caused it to fail readiness checks and return HTTP 500 errors on the `/orders` endpoint. The application logs explicitly stated: "RELEASE=v2 failing readiness and returning 500s on /orders (regression introduced in this release; previous good release was v1)". The payments service was correctly running v1.
postmortem: The `web` service was deployed with RELEASE=v2, which contained a regression causing it to fail readiness/health checks and return HTTP 500 errors on the `/orders` (checkout) endpoint. The application itself logged the issue explicitly: "RELEASE=v2 failing readiness and returning 500s on /orders (regression introduced in this release; previous good release was v1)". The container was running and the database was unaffected — the failure was purely an application-level regression in v2. The fix was a rollback to RELEASE=v1 by recreating the container with the correct environment variable. No data loss occurred. To prevent recurrence, the deployment pipeline should include automated smoke tests against the `/orders` endpoint and a canary/rollback mechanism that detects health-check failures and automatically reverts to the previous known-good release.
